name: _is_service_endpoint_decorator

doc: Return qualified name string if func looks like tk/ttk constructor, else None.

Returns the default safety contracts.
Hardcoded here to remove dependency on external contract files.

name: cmd_pipeline
doc: Run the full pipeline: tokenize → chunk → embed → reverse.

name: cmd_train
doc: Train the BDVE model from a text file.

name: _assign_category
doc: Map a scored node to its budget category.

name: _item_weight
doc: Compute the packing weight of a content block.

name: _node_density
doc: Compute packing density = score / item_weight.

name: config_for_mode
doc: Create a fresh CompositionConfig for the given runtime mode.

name: _bpe_encode
doc:
Encode text to token IDs using BPE vocab + merges (no provider needed).

This standalone encoder replicates the provider's encoding logic so that
the training pipeline can produce token ID streams *before* embeddings
exist (bootstrapping).

name: _progress
doc: Call progress callback if provided.

name: chunk
doc: Tokenise text and split into budget-bounded hunks.

name: embed_hunk
doc:
Produce an embedding vector for a single hunk.

Uses real matrix lookup + mean pooling if a model is trained,
otherwise returns a placeholder vector.

name: is_trained
doc: Whether a trained model is loaded and ready.

name: load_if_available
doc:
Try to load provider from saved artifacts on disk.

Returns True if a provider was successfully loaded, False otherwise.
Call this at startup so that previously trained models are available
immediately without re-training.

name: reverse_vector
doc:
Find the nearest tokens to a given vector.

Uses real cosine-similarity search if a model is trained,
otherwise returns placeholder results.

name: tokenize
doc:
Convert raw text into BPE tokens.

Uses real BPE encoding if a model is trained, otherwise returns
placeholder tokens.

name: train_from_file
doc:
Full training pipeline: file -> tokenizer -> co-occurrence -> NPMI -> SVD -> provider.

Parameters
----------
file_path : str
    Path to a text file to train on.
vocab_size : int
    Target BPE vocabulary size.
embedding_dims : int
    Number of SVD dimensions (columns in the embedding matrix).
window_size : int
    Co-occurrence sliding window width.
on_progress : callable, optional
    Callback receiving progress messages (for UI status bar).

name: extract_text_from_html
doc: Best-effort HTML to text extraction with stdlib fallback.

name: extract_text_from_pdf
doc: Best-effort PDF text extraction.

name: extract_service_schema
doc:
Scans a decorated Service Class and returns a JSON-serializable schema
of its metadata and all its exposed endpoints.

This is what the AI Agent uses to 'read' the manual.

name: service_endpoint
doc:
Method Decorator.
Defines the 'Socket' that the AI Architect can plug into.

:param inputs: Dict of {arg_name: type_string} (e.g. {"query": "str"})
:param outputs: Dict of {return_name: type_string}
:param description: What the endpoint does
:param tags: List of categories (e.g. ["read", "write"])
:param side_effects: List of side effects (e.g. ["filesystem:write", "db:write"])
:param mode: "sync" or "async" (informational unless your runtime uses it)

name: service_metadata
doc:
Class Decorator.
Labels a Microservice class with high-level metadata for the Catalog.

Dependency semantics:
  - internal_dependencies: local modules and/or other microservice modules that must be shipped with an app
  - external_dependencies: third-party pip packages (requirements.txt)
  - dependencies (legacy): treated as external_dependencies when external_dependencies is not provided

name: _isolated_worker
doc:
Entry point for the child process.
Configures a logging handler to send records back to the parent.
Note: Must remain top-level for multiprocessing pickling compatibility.

name: blake3_hasher_reference
doc: Python reference. WASM version runs same logic in C.

name: interval_query_reference
doc: Return span IDs that overlap with [query_start, query_end].

name: compute_redundancy_penalties
doc:
Compute per-candidate redundancy penalty factors.

For each candidate, checks overlap with already-selected items on:
  - same source_ref
  - shared tag cluster
  - repeated (category, source) combinations

Args:
    candidates: Nodes being considered for packing.
    selected:   Nodes already packed into the bag.
    strength:   Controls the maximum penalty cap.

Returns:
    Dict mapping candidate node_id → penalty_factor in [0, cap).
    Missing IDs have zero penalty.

name: _cosine_similarity
doc: Compute cosine similarity between two float vectors.

name: _pack_vector
doc: Pack a list of floats into a compact binary blob (float32).

name: _unpack_vector
doc: Unpack a binary blob back into a list of floats.

name: token_overlap
doc:
Count the number of shared tokens between two sets.

Args:
    a: First token set.
    b: Second token set.

Returns:
    Integer count of common tokens.

name: tokenize
doc:
Extract unique tokens from text.

Tokens are lowercase sequences of [a-z0-9_] with length >= 3.

Args:
    text: Input string (any casing).

Returns:
    Frozen set of unique token strings.

name: find_get_health_position

Find the line index of 'def get_health' inside the class.
We insert register() immediately before get_health() so it stays
grouped with the standard interface methods.

name: map_project

name: _args_sig

name: _expr_to_str

name: _extract_bind_event

name: _is_widget_ctor_call

name: _index_functions

Returns:
    symbol_key -> (path, node)
symbol_key formats:
    - modulepath::funcname
    - modulepath::ClassName.method

name: _resolve_called_name
doc:
called may be:
    - foo
    - self.foo
    - mod.foo
Resolve conservatively to a unique symbol in index.

name: predicate
doc:
Return a no-arg callable usable as cancel() injection in services:
    cancel = token.predicate()
    if cancel(): ...

name: _read_text_head
doc: Reads up to read_bytes_limit bytes, decodes as utf-8 with replacement.

name: find_candidates
doc: Returns ranked candidates (highest score first).

name: normalize
doc: Convert err into NormalizedError. Never raises.

name: _match_path
doc:
Path matching:
- If rooted: only match from root (we already have rel_posix)
- Else:
    - If pat contains "/" we allow matching at any depth by trying:
        - exact full-path match
        - "**/pat" match

name: _rule_matches
doc:
A pragmatic matcher:
- If rule contains "/" => match against full relative path (posix)
- Else => match against basename in any directory (like gitignore)
- If rule.rooted => anchor at repo root (i.e., compare from start)

name: is_ignored
doc: Returns True if gitignore rules ignore this path.

name: load
doc:
Load ignore rules from known locations + any extra ignore files.
Safe to call multiple times; it replaces existing rules.

name: predicate

    (abs_path: Path, is_dir: bool) -> keep?

name: build_prompt

parameters.name: goal
